[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 志怪 - Refers to the recording of strange or supernatural events, a genre of literature.
  • 志常 - Refers to the recording of ordinary or everyday events.
  • 幽诡之事 - Means 'mysterious and strange matters.'
  • 变异之来 - Means 'the occurrence of unusual events.'
  • 卒然之顷 - Means 'suddenly' or 'unexpectedly.'
  • 趋避 - Means 'to avoid or evade.'
  • 劝惩 - Means 'to encourage and punish.'
  • 恍语惚说 - Means 'vague and uncertain speech.'
  • 夺目警耳 - Means 'to catch the eye and alarm the ear.'
  • 吾侪 - Means 'we' or 'us,' referring to the author and his peers.
  • 芜鄙 - Means 'vulgar and crude.'
  • 班洪 - Means 'to rank with' or 'to compare with' (referring to Hong, the author of *Yijian Zhi*).
  • 高论者 - Means 'those with lofty opinions.'
  • 罪其缪悠 - Means 'to blame their absurdity.'
  • 一委之以不语常之失 - Means 'to entirely dismiss it as a failure to speak of ordinary things.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洪野处夷坚志 - Refers to the work *Yijian Zhi* by Hong, a famous collection of strange tales.
  • 四百二十卷 - Indicates the extensive length of *Yijian Zhi*, which consists of 420 volumes.
  • 吾书虽芜鄙 - The author modestly describes his own work as 'vulgar and crude.'
  • 昔洪野处夷坚志至于四百二十卷之富 - Provides a historical reference to the extensive nature of *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 吾以是知吾书虽芜鄙不敢班洪亦姑从吾所好耳 - Expresses the author's modesty and his personal interest in writing about strange tales.
  • 若有高论者罪其缪悠而一委之以不语常之失则洪书当先吾而废吾何忧哉 - Suggests that if critics blame his work for its absurdity and dismiss it as a failure to speak of ordinary things, then *Yijian Zhi* should be dismissed first, and he has no reason to worry.

Proper Nouns

  • 洪 - Refers to Hong, the author of *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 夷坚志 - The title of Hong's work, *Yijian Zhi*.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少时相得出入恒俱 - Literally means 'in their youth, they often went out and in together,' indicating a close friendship or partnership.
  • 黎明同入郡学傍一居人寝门内 - Literally means 'at dawn, they entered the prefectural school, and a resident living near the school's door.'
  • 闻街上枷锁之声窥之见狱卒自变量囚南行 - Literally means 'they heard the sound of shackles on the street and looked to see a jailer leading several prisoners southward.'
  • 俄而卒及囚皆仓皇曰丞相来宜急避之遂皆入古庙而没 - Literally means 'soon, the jailer and prisoners all hurriedly said that the chancellor was coming and should quickly avoid it, so they all entered an ancient temple and disappeared.'
  • 居人念囚不应行此路且讶丞相之语因坐以伺之则二公随至矣 - Literally means 'the resident thought that prisoners should not be taking this road and was surprised by the chancellor's words, so he sat and waited, and the two gentlemen soon arrived.'
  • 忽隐隐闻冢上羣鸟鸣噪有人见而怪之至墓旁闻墓中号呼之声告萧氏家乃急发冢出之 - Literally means 'suddenly, he heard the faint sound of birds chirping and cawing on the tomb, and someone saw and found it strange, so they went to the side of the tomb and heard the sound of shouting from inside the tomb, and they informed the Xiao family, who then quickly opened the tomb and brought her out.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈僖敏公 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Ming or Qing dynasty.
  • 太子太保 - A high-ranking official position, one of the Three Ducal Ministers in the imperial court, responsible for advising the heir apparent.
  • 郡学 - Refers to a local school or academy, often the highest level of education in a prefecture.
  • 冥府 - Refers to the underworld or the realm of the dead, a common concept in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 王者三人 - Likely refers to three officials or judges in the underworld, similar to the concept of judges in the afterlife in various cultures.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈僖敏公 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Ming or Qing dynasty.
  • 太子太保 - A high-ranking official position, one of the Three Ducal Ministers in the imperial court, responsible for advising the heir apparent.
  • 俞公士悦 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Ming or Qing dynasty.
  • 萧都宪 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Ming or Qing dynasty.
  • 刘氏 - The name of a woman, likely the wife of萧都宪.
  • 还娘 - A nickname or given name, likely a personal name of the woman.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 疫疠盛行 - Literal meaning: 'epidemic diseases are rampant'; indicates a widespread outbreak of infectious diseases.
  • 一家七人同死无孑遗 - Literal meaning: 'a family of seven died together, leaving no survivors'; emphasizes the tragic event where an entire family perished.
  • 无人敛 - Literal meaning: 'nobody to bury'; highlights the lack of resources or people to handle the burials.
  • 赤手不持纤价 - Literal meaning: 'with empty hands and not holding any money'; indicates the old man's destitute condition.
  • 汝但载我并棺到家当还汝也 - Literal meaning: 'you just carry me and the coffins home, and I will pay you'; a promise made by the old man.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 成化壬寅春 - Specific date in the Chinese calendar, referring to the spring of the year Renyin during the Chenghua era (1465).
  • 吴中 - Region around Suzhou, a center of culture and economy during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 疫疠 - Refers to epidemics, which were common and often devastating during this period.
  • 田野 - Refers to rural areas, which were often more vulnerable to epidemics due to limited medical resources.
  • 五澴泾 - Specific location in the Wu region, possibly a small town or village.
  • 函人 - Refers to a coffin maker or undertaker, a profession that would have been essential during times of high mortality.

Proper Nouns

  • 成化 - Era name of the Ming Dynasty, referring to the reign of Emperor Yingzong.
  • 壬寅 - Stem-branch year designation for 1465.
  • 吴中 - Region around Suzhou.
  • 五澴泾 - Specific location in the Wu region.
  • 函人 - Term for a coffin maker or undertaker.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天堕草船 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'a grass boat falling from the sky'; describes an unusual or inexplicable event.
  • 松江城西 - Refers to the western part of Songjiang City, indicating a specific location.
  • 董仲俯 - Name of a person, likely a historical figure or a character in the story.
  • 以敦厚穪成化 - Phrase meaning 'with virtue and integrity to achieve harmony' or 'to complete transformation'; exact meaning unclear without more context.
  • 天宇澄霁 - Means 'the sky is clear and bright,' describing a beautiful day.
  • 皎无纤云 - Means 'bright and cloudless,' further emphasizing the clear sky.
  • 众见空中有小船从东而西又转而东堕 - Describes the unusual event of a small boat being seen in the sky, moving from east to west and then back to the east, and finally falling.
  • 仲俯楼上观者塞道 - Means 'people on the street were packed to watch from the upper floor of Zhongfu's house.'
  • 细视之乃茭草所缚者 - Means 'upon closer inspection, it was found to be made of reeds and grass.'
  • 方患耳瞶 - Means 'he was suffering from deafness in his ears.'
  • 不大惊但曰此船来载我耳 - Means 'he was not greatly alarmed but said, 'This boat has come to take me.''
  • 不久果卒 - Means 'he soon died as predicted.'
  • 白犬怪 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the white dog's curse' or 'the white dog's anomaly.'
  • 昆山一民家子 - Refers to a young man from a common family in Kunshan.
  • 由是得瘵疾 - Means 'as a result, he contracted a wasting disease.'
  • 医疗莫愈 - Means 'medical treatment could not cure him.'
  • 子犹秘其事 - Means 'the son still kept the matter secret.'
  • 坐肆中 - Means 'sitting in a shop.'
  • 道士过之 - Means 'a Taoist priest passed by.'
  • 妖气甚浓 - Means 'the aura of evil is very strong.'
  • 言毕遂去 - Means 'after saying this, he left.'
  • 其父闻之乃谓子曰 - Means 'his father heard this and then told his son.'
  • 吾儿不言即当死矣 - Means 'if you don't speak, you will die.'
  • 言之吾不责汝也 - Means 'if you tell me, I won't blame you.'
  • 子乃以实告父 - Means 'the son then told his father the truth.'
  • 吾以剪刀与汝伺其来但剪其衣便可验治 - Means 'I will give you a pair of scissors. When she comes, just cut her clothes, and you can verify the cure.'
  • 子受命惟谨 - Means 'the son accepted the command with great care.'
  • 其夜妇至子密剪其衣亟厉声斥之 - Means 'that night, the woman came, and the son secretly cut her clothes and shouted at her fiercely.'
  • 妇遂去 - Means 'the woman then left.'
  • 旦视之皆白毛也 - Means 'in the morning, it was found that she had white hair.'
  • 父见僯家一牝白犬毛有缺处 - Means 'the father saw a white female dog at the neighbor's house with a missing patch of fur.'
  • 因共杖杀之 - Means 'they then beat the dog to death together.'
  • 子疾随差 - Means 'the son's illness then improved.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 松江 - Refers to Songjiang, a city in present-day Shanghai, China.
  • 董仲俯 - A historical figure or a character in the story, known for his virtue and integrity.
  • 成化 - Refers to the Chenghua era, a period during the Ming Dynasty (1465-1487).
  • 昆山 - Refers to Kunshan, a city in present-day Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 瘵疾 - Refers to a wasting disease, often associated with tuberculosis in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest, a religious figure in Chinese tradition.
  • 妖气 - Refers to an aura of evil or supernatural influence, often associated with spirits or ghosts in Chinese folklore.

Proper Nouns

  • 董仲俯 - Name of a person, likely a historical figure or a character in the story.
  • 昆山 - Refers to Kunshan, a city in present-day Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 成化 - Refers to the Chenghua era, a period during the Ming Dynasty (1465-1487).

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 朝罢趋归 - Literally 'after the court session, hurrying to return home,' indicating the routine of officials returning home after work.
  • 径至 - Means 'directly to' or 'straight to,' suggesting a direct route.
  • 拱揖数四 - A formal gesture of respect, performed multiple times.
  • 倐然觧带 - Suddenly unfastening one's belt, a preparatory action before entering water.
  • 随涌身而下 - Jumping into the water, implying a sudden and dramatic action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 成化中 - The Chenghua period (1465-1487) of the Ming dynasty, known for its cultural and artistic achievements.
  • 宗人府 - The Imperial Clan Court, responsible for managing the royal family's affairs, reflecting the importance of the imperial lineage.
  • 古井 - Often associated with supernatural stories in Chinese folklore.
  • 贡尚书 - A high-ranking official, specifically a Grand Secretary.
  • 罗洗马 - Luo Xima, a title referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 李侍讲 - Li Shijiang, a title referring to a high-ranking official.
  • 南畿秋试 - The imperial examination held in the southern region during the autumn.
  • 首选 - The top choice or selection in the imperial examination, indicating exceptional talent.
  • 宁国贡钦 - A person named Gong Qin from Ningguo.
  • 元尚书师泰 - A former Grand Secretary named Shi Tai.
  • 相城村人 - A villager from Xiangcheng.
  • 夜启门 - Opening the door at night, often associated with mysterious or supernatural events.

Proper Nouns

  • 冯副使 - Feng Fushi, a high-ranking official.
  • 成化 - The Chenghua period of the Ming dynasty.
  • 宗人府 - The Imperial Clan Court.
  • 罗洗马 - Luo Xima, a high-ranking official.
  • 李侍讲 - Li Shijiang, a high-ranking official.
  • 南畿 - The southern region of China.
  • 贡尚书 - A high-ranking official.
  • 宁国贡钦 - Gong Qin from Ningguo.
  • 元尚书师泰 - Shi Tai, a former high-ranking official.
  • 相城村人 - A villager from Xiangcheng.
  • 王捉鬼 - Wang Zhuogui, a name given to the villager.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 海神请读书人 - Supernatural event where a sea deity summons a person.
  • 倚门少立 - Lean against the door and stand for a moment, indicating a brief pause.
  • 忽向东疾奔 - Suddenly rushed eastward, describing a rapid movement.
  • 瞬息不见 - Disappeared in an instant, emphasizing the speed of the event.
  • 折径去矣 - Broke off and went away, describing the tree breaking and the person continuing their journey.
  • 直望海而趋 - Rushed straight toward the sea, indicating a direct path.
  • 入海矣 - Entered the sea, describing the final destination.
  • 卧前村土地堂 - Lay in the village land temple, indicating a place of rest.
  • 伏地而睡 - Lay on the ground and slept, describing the state of the person.
  • 戴软趐唐帽 - Wearing a soft, wide-brimmed hat, describing the appearance.
  • 衣绿袍 - Wearing a green robe, describing the attire.
  • 东黄金带 - A golden belt on the left side, describing the accessory.
  • 骑白马 - Riding a white horse, describing the mode of transportation.
  • 或虾或螺或鳖或鱼 - Some were shrimp, some were snails, some were turtles, and some were fish, describing the followers.
  • 命从者羣挟之东行 - Ordered his followers to carry him eastward, describing the action.
  • 虽大呼不可得止也 - Even if he shouted, he could not stop, indicating the inability to halt the journey.
  • 眼开能运动 - Opened his eyes and could move, describing the recovery.
  • 汤液灌之 - Fed him with soup, describing the care given.
  • 一日始复 - Returned to normal after one day, indicating the duration of recovery.
  • 无有也 - There was no one, indicating the absence of the person.
  • 痛骇 - Painfully frightened, describing the emotional state.
  • 丐儿来报 - A beggar came to report, indicating the messenger.
  • 昨夜 - Last night, indicating the time.
  • 昏昏然不省娄页中恶者 - Dazed and unconscious, suffering from a severe illness, describing the condition.
  • 幸而眼开能运动 - Fortunately, he opened his eyes and could move, indicating the recovery.
  • 汤液灌之 - Fed him with soup, describing the care given.
  • 一日始复 - Returned to normal after one day, indicating the duration of recovery.
  • 众问其详 - Everyone asked for details, indicating the curiosity.
  • 儿方倚门 - The child was leaning against the door, describing the initial state.
  • 见一少年从东来 - Saw a young man coming from the east, describing the encounter.
  • 貌甚娟秀 - Had a very beautiful appearance, describing the person.
  • 亦莹皎如雪 - Also as bright and white as snow, describing the appearance.
  • 从后者可三十人 - Followed by about thirty people, describing the entourage.
  • 皆人身而首则或虾或螺或鳖或鱼 - All had human bodies but heads that were either shrimp, snails, turtles, or fish, describing the followers.
  • 少年见我即命从者羣挟之东行 - The young man saw me and ordered his followers to carry me eastward, describing the action.
  • 虽大呼不可得止也 - Even if I shouted, I could not stop, indicating the inability to halt the journey.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉定 - A county in Jiangsu Province, China, during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 东门外 - Refers to the eastern gate of the city, indicating a specific location.
  • 朱外郎 - Refers to a person of the Zhu family, likely a government official.
  • 外傅 - Refers to a private tutor, indicating the educational context.
  • 海神 - Refers to a sea deity, indicating a supernatural element.
  • 土地堂 - Refers to a village land temple, indicating a place of worship.
  • 软趐唐帽 - A type of hat worn during the Ming Dynasty, indicating the period.
  • 绿袍 - A type of robe, indicating the attire of the time.
  • 东黄金带 - A golden belt worn on the left side, indicating the accessory.
  • 白马 - A white horse, indicating the mode of transportation.
  • 汤液 - Refers to a type of soup, indicating the care given.
  • 娄页中恶者 - Refers to a severe illness, indicating the condition.
  • 丐儿 - Refers to a beggar, indicating the messenger.
  • 昨夜 - Refers to the time, indicating when the event occurred.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至岸视海中一道水开遂成路接沙 - Descriptive phrase indicating the appearance of a path through the sea, which is likely a supernatural or magical occurrence.
  • 众皆拥上路四望弥漫俄顷至一城郭 - Phrase describing the crowd's reaction and the sudden appearance of a city, emphasizing the rapidity of the event.
  • 入至大宫阙朱门华屋弘敞焕烂 - Descriptive language highlighting the grandeur and beauty of the palace, with red doors and splendid buildings.
  • 少年止于门外只令阍者通谒曰奉命请读书人至其阍者亦鱼鳖之属受语趋入少顷复出命他吏引入至一殿下其殿极高广都作白色似蠵蛎之壳所光采照耀恍惚夺目不能正视 - Complex sentence describing the interaction with the gatekeeper and the subsequent introduction to the palace, emphasizing the opulence and grandeur.
  • 吏命入殿王者坐殿上其貌巳老须鬓雪白其冠亦娄页唐帽身披白袍通剌金纹腰横白玉带问我曰汝觧作文章乎对曰不能曰然则何能曰只会作对王曰我只要作文章者今汝不能无用汝也因命左右引入学馆一观即放回左右遂引入东偏一室见一童子可数岁韶秀特异旁吏云王欲请读书人教此子耳少顷复引见王王命仍遣人送去众复引出前少年尚待于门得传命因复乘马命众扶之归至土地庙前庙神出迎甚恭少年即以儿付之土地即收儿宿儿后亦无他今尚在也事在成化十九年 - Detailed narrative describing the encounter with the king, the interaction with the officials, and the final outcome, including the child's fate.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 成化十九年 - Refers to the year 1483 during the Ming dynasty, providing a specific historical context.
  • 王 - Refers to a king or ruler, likely a supernatural or mythical figure given the context of the story.
  • 学馆 - Refers to a school or educational institution, indicating the importance of education in the narrative.
  • 土地庙 - Refers to a temple dedicated to the local land god, suggesting a blend of religious and secular authority.

Proper Nouns

  • 成化十九年 - The year 1483 during the Ming dynasty.
  • 王 - A king or ruler, likely a supernatural or mythical figure.
  • 土地庙 - A temple dedicated to the local land god.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雷书 - Refers to a text or record related to thunder or lightning phenomena.
  • 丁未五六月 - Refers to the months of May and June in the year of Ding Wei (丁未), a sexagenary cycle year in the Chinese calendar.
  • 赤地千里 - A vivid expression meaning 'thousand miles of red earth,' describing a vast area of land turned barren or scorched.
  • 申时 - Refers to the time period from 3 PM to 5 PM, a specific time of day in traditional Chinese timekeeping.
  • 合抱 - A measure of the tree's girth, meaning it can be encircled by two arms.
  • 雷击一大枝 - Describes a branch of the tree being struck by lightning.
  • 画纹如指阔 - Describes the width of the lines on the ground as being like the width of a finger.
  • 深入土几寸 - Indicates that the lines were deeply embedded in the soil.
  • 子乃言非篆非草 - A phrase indicating that the writing is neither seal script (篆) nor cursive script (草).
  • 虽经雨水冲灌而不湮没 - Means that despite being washed by rain, the writing did not disappear.
  • 天神书也 - A phrase indicating that the writing was done by a heavenly deity.
  • 次日 - Refers to the next day.
  • 予始知之 - Means 'I first learned about it.'
  • 以纸摹得其文 - Indicates that the text was copied onto paper.
  • 妓乘鱼 - A title or heading that likely refers to a story or anecdote involving a courtesan and a fish.
  • 南京教坊 - Refers to a music bureau or entertainment establishment in Nanjing.
  • 盐商 - Refers to a salt merchant, a common term for wealthy traders in traditional Chinese society.
  • 行货广陵 - Means 'traveling to Guangling,' referring to the city of Yangzhou.
  • 汝能待我乎 - A question asking 'Can you wait for me?'
  • 买舟独归 - Means 'to buy a boat and return alone.'
  • 数取金令舟人碎之 - Describes the action of taking gold and instructing the boatman to break it.
  • 买物 - Means 'to buy things.'
  • 舟人窃念可图 - Indicates that the boatman secretly thought he could gain something from this.
  • 江中夜静 - Refers to a quiet night on the river.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丁未 - A sexagenary cycle year in the Chinese calendar, corresponding to a specific year in the 60-year cycle.
  • 崇义庵 - A specific temple or shrine, possibly with historical or cultural significance.
  • 梓树 - A type of tree with cultural significance in Chinese literature and art.
  • 雷书 - A term that may refer to a specific text or tradition related to thunder and lightning.
  • 南京教坊 - A music bureau or entertainment establishment in Nanjing, reflecting the cultural and social life of the time.
  • 盐商 - Wealthy merchants who played a significant role in the economy and social structure of traditional China.
  • 广陵 - An ancient name for Yangzhou, a city with a rich cultural and historical background.
  • 妓 - Refers to a courtesan, a woman who was trained in music, dance, and other arts and often served as a companion to wealthy men.
  • 舟人 - Refers to a boatman or sailor, a common term in traditional Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 崇义庵 - A specific temple or shrine.
  • 南京 - The city of Nanjing.
  • 广陵 - An ancient name for Yangzhou.
  • 盐商 - A specific type of merchant.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 投妓水中尽有其货 - Literal phrase meaning 'the courtesan threw herself into the water, and all her goods were there,' describing a situation where the courtesan's belongings were found in the water.
  • 径行去 - Means 'went straight away' or 'left immediately.'
  • 日将出至江阴一村 - Means 'as the sun was rising, they arrived at a village in Jiangyin.'
  • 大呼救我 - Means 'shouted for help.'
  • 村中集人视之 - Means 'people in the village gathered to see what happened.'
  • 顷刻抵岸 - Means 'in a moment, they reached the shore.'
  • 下众扶掖而起 - Means 'the crowd helped her up from the water.'
  • 视所乘乃大鱼 - Means 'they saw that she was riding on a large fish.'
  • 计此时贼尚未见也 - Means 'it was calculated that the thieves had not yet seen this.'
  • 官如其言急往捡诸船 - Means 'the official acted as she said and quickly went to check all the boats.'
  • 果获盗寘之于法而觧妓还院 - Means 'he indeed caught the thieves and punished them according to the law, then sent the courtesan back to her quarters.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 江阴 - A city in Jiangsu Province, China, known for its historical significance.
  • 红罗鞋一只上绣石楼花并绣主腰一件 - Refers to a red silk shoe embroidered with stone tower flowers and a main waistband, which are specific items that were left behind.
  • 龙虎山道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest from Longhu Mountain, a famous Taoist center in China.
  • 朱砂符 - Refers to a talisman made of cinnabar (a red mineral), used in Taoist rituals for protection or exorcism.
  • 柳树根下 - Refers to the root of a willow tree, where the talisman was placed.
  • 鳣 - A type of large fish, often associated with auspiciousness in Chinese culture.
  • 寘之于法 - Means 'punished according to the law,' indicating a formal legal process.
  • 觧妓还院 - Means 'sent the courtesan back to her quarters,' indicating the restoration of her status.

Proper Nouns

  • 保保 - The name of the child.
